prerequisites: A Plaid Dashboard account (sign up at dashboard.plaid.com). After signup and accepting the terms, Plaid issues your client_id and per-environment secret.
Your client_id (the private identifier for your team), provided in the Dashboard under Developers > Keys.
Your Plaid secret key (one per environment), provided in the same API Keys page. In this connector the secret is supplied via the api_key field.

An access_token for the Item you want to read. Obtain it by running the Plaid Link flow and exchanging the resulting public_token via /item/public_token/exchange. The access_token does not expire and must be stored securely.

A target Plaid environment (plaid_env), either sandbox for test data or production for live data. Production requires separate access approval through the Dashboard, and Items cannot be moved between environments.

authMethods.name: Plaid app credentials (client_id + secret)
authMethods.summary: Almost all Plaid API endpoints require your client_id together with your secret (supplied here through the api_key field). Plaid accepts them in the request body or as the PLAID-CLIENT-ID and PLAID-SECRET headers. Both are issued per team in the Plaid Dashboard, with a distinct secret for each environment.

authMethods.name: Item access_token
authMethods.summary: Most data endpoints also require an access_token that uniquely identifies a single Item (a user's login at a financial institution). It is created by exchanging a Plaid Link public_token via /item/public_token/exchange, never expires, and must be stored securely. Provide it in the access_token field.

setupSteps.title: Create a Plaid Dashboard account
setupSteps.body: Sign up at dashboard.plaid.com and accept Plaid's terms. After signup, Plaid provisions a live client_id and a secret for your team.

setupSteps.title: Copy your client_id and secret
setupSteps.body: Open Developers > Keys in the Dashboard. Copy the client_id (private identifier for your team) into the client_id field. Copy the secret for the environment you are targeting into the api_key field. There is a separate secret per environment.

setupSteps.title: Choose the Plaid environment
setupSteps.body: Set plaid_env to the environment you want to connect to: sandbox for life-like test data, or production for live data. Production access must be requested through the Dashboard, and Items cannot be moved between environments.

setupSteps.title: Generate an access_token for the Item
setupSteps.body: Run the Plaid Link flow: call /link/token/create, complete Link as the end user to receive a public_token (which expires after 30 minutes), then call /item/public_token/exchange to obtain the permanent access_token. Paste that access_token into the access_token field.

setupSteps.title: Set the optional start date
setupSteps.body: Optionally set start_date to bound how far back transaction-style data is pulled. Leave it empty to use the connector default.

sources.title: Plaid Quickstart (API keys, Link, token exchange)
